title: Memoize only proven hot paths
description: Apply useMemo/useCallback/React.memo to fix a measured render cost or a referential-identity break, not as a blanket default.
body: Use when a profiler shows a component or computation is a real render hotspot, or when an unstable reference breaks a downstream memo or effect dependency. Do not wrap every value and callback by default; memoization has its own allocation and comparison cost and can hide bugs. Actions: profile first, identify the specific reference or expensive compute, then memoize that one path and confirm the dependency array is exhaustive. Validation signals include a render trace showing fewer commits and a passing test for the memoized output. Risk boundary: a wrong or missing dependency in useMemo/useCallback yields stale closures; if correctness is unclear, prefer no memoization over a subtly stale one.
